title: An adjusting knob
abstract: The invention relates to an adjusting knob without any mechanical adjusting member, wherein the distance between the components changes depending on the turning of a movable member and a function and/or the value of a variable, which is/are desired to be controlled, is/are enabled to be adjusted according to said distance differences between the components, wherein said adjusting knob provides a feedback to its user depending on the change in the function and/or the variable value and wherein the precision of said adjusting knob is able to be set. More particularly, the invention relates to an adjusting knob comprising at least one sensor (3) and a movable surface that moves towards or away from said sensor (3) and at least one feedback member (4).
claims: Claims 1. An adjusting knob enabling at least one function and/or variable of a system to be adjusted characterized in that it comprises â¢ at least one sensor (3) with a fixed position and â¢ at least one protrusion, i.e. a movable component protrusion (22), which moves towards or away from the sensor (3) depending on the direction of the force applied by the user. 2. An adjusting knob according to Claim 1 characterized in that it comprises at least one body (1) with a fixed position, on which the other components are located, and at least one movable component (2), which is capable of moving on said body (1). 3. An adjusting knob according to Claim 2 characterized in that it comprises, on the body (1), a movable component (2) that is capable of freely rotating about its central axis. 4. An adjusting knob according to Claim 2 characterized in that the sensor (3) is in a state fixed on the body (1). 5. An adjusting knob according to Claim 2 characterized in that it comprises at least one spring (5), which has one end connected to the body (1) and another end connected to the movable component (2), stores the force applied to the movable component (2) by way of compressing or stretching in response to said force and enables the movable component (2) to return to its initial position when said movable component (2) is released. 6. An adjusting knob according to Claim 2 characterized in that the body (1) comprises at least one body connecting piece (11) to which at least one sensor (3) and/or one end of at least one spring (5) is/are fixed. 7. An adjusting knob according to Claim 2 characterized in that the movable component (2) comprises at least one movable component connecting piece (21), which enables the connection of one end of at least one spring (5). 8. An adjusting knob according to Claim 6 or 7 characterized in that it comprises a first spring (5), one end of which is connected to a first body connecting piece (11) and the other end of which is connected to a first movable component connecting piece (21), and a second spring, one end of which is connected to a second body connecting piece (11) and the other end of which is connected to a second movable component connecting piece (21). 9. An adjusting knob according to Claim 1 or 6 characterized in that it comprises a first sensor (3) fixed on a first body connecting piece (11) and a second sensor (3) fixed on a second body connecting piece (11). 10. An adjusting knob according to Claim 1 characterized in that it comprises a control unit, which adjusts a function and/or the value of a variable of at least one device intended to be controlled, depending on the position of the movable component protrusion (22) relative to the sensor (3). 11. An adjusting knob according to Claim 1 characterized in that it has a feedback member (4), which provides a feedback every time the protrusion, moving towards or away from the sensor (3) depending on the direction of the force applied by the user, moves towards or away from the sensor (3). 12. An adjusting knob according to Claim 11 characterized in that the feedback member (4) is a feedback member (4) providing an audible, visual and/or physical feedback. 13. An adjusting knob according to Claim 12 characterized in that the feedback member (4) is a vibration motor. 14. An adjusting knob according to Claim 2 or 11 characterized in that the feedback member (4) is located on the body (1). 15. An adjusting knob according to Claim 10 characterized in that the control unit is a control unit capable of communicating in a cabled and/or wireless manner with the device, a variable or function of which is desired to be adjusted. 16. An adjusting knob according to Claim 15 characterized in that the control unit comprises a wireless connection member, which enables said control unit to establish wireless connection. 17. An adjusting knob according to Claim 10 characterized in that the control unit is a control unit capable of establishing communication simultaneously with more than one device.
